heading: User fees for reports, publications, and software
label: 2242a User fees for reports, publications, and software
notes: Amendments1990—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 101–6241985—, in amending section generally, divided existing provisions into subsecs. (a), (b), and (c) and inserted references to software programs and electronic publications.Pub. L. 99–198
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Dec. 22, 1981section 1801 of Pub. L. 97–98section 4301 of this title
amendments: Amendments1990—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 101–6241985—, in amending section generally, divided existing provisions into subsecs. (a), (b), and (c) and inserted references to software programs and electronic publications.Pub. L. 99–198

children.text: furnish, on request, copies of software programs, pamphlets, reports, or other publications, regardless of their form, including electronic publications, prepared in the Department of Agriculture in carrying out any of its missions or programs; and
charge such fees therefor as the Secretary determines are reasonable.

children.heading: Consistency of charges with provisions ofsection 9701 of title 31
children.label: b Consistency of charges with provisions ofsection 9701 of title 31
children.text: The imposition of such charges shall be consistent with .section 9701 of title 31

children.heading: Use and disposition of moneys
children.label: c Use and disposition of moneys

children.text: shall be available until expended to pay directly the costs of such work, services, software programs, pamphlets, reports, or publications; and
may be credited to appropriations or funds that incur such costs.

children.heading: Investment
children.label: d Investment
children.text: Any fees collected, late payment penalties, and interest earned shall be credited to the account referred to in this section and may be invested by the Secretary of Agriculture in insured or fully-collateralized interest-bearing accounts or, at the discretion of the Secretary of Agriculture, by the Secretary of the Treasury in United States Government debt instruments. Fees and charges, including late payment penalties and interest earned from the investment of such funds shall be credited to such account.
